artPrompt: A shadowed back room where a goblin hunches over an overflowing blank ballot box under a single amber lamp, slyly sorting smudged slips into a favored pile, cigar smoke curling around his sly grin; the wobbling table and ink-black corners blur into smoky haze, the ongoing quiet corruption of the count made into a dreamy, melancholic tableau, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: Rigged Recount Racket
text:
Whenever a player votes or a vote is held, you may sacrifice a Favor token. If you do, you cast that player's vote however you like, then quietly ask for a recount. The recount agrees with you.
The ballots all came back marked in the same goblin handwriting. Nobody mentioned it.
flavorText: Democracy is too important to leave to the voters.
